data.title: Truth Social Funds registers with SEC for two digital asset ETFs: Truth Social Cronos Yield Maximizer ETF and Truth Social Bitcoin ETF.
data.description: In Sarasota, Florida, on February 13, 2026, Truth Social Funds disclosed their submission of a registration statement to the U.S. Securities and Exchange Commission (SEC) for two exchange-traded funds. The funds in question are the Truth Social Cronos Yield Maximizer ETF and the Truth Social Bitcoin.

data.title: Vivint introduces Vivint for Builders, simplifying smart home setup and aiming to enhance homeowner experience.
data.description: In Lehi, Utah, Vivint, a subsidiary of NRG, the top US energy-focused security and smart home firm, has unveiled Vivint for Builders, a comprehensive smart home security offering tailored to streamline the builder process and enhance homeowner satisfaction. In the current housing industry, prospective homeowners increasingly demand smart home features for security, comfort, and convenience. Vivint for Builders provides builders with a seamless smart home solution, catering to this growing trend in the market.

data.title: KBRA assigns ratings to $475.8M mortgage-backed notes by NRMLT 2026-NQM3. 10 classes rated in non-prime RMBS transaction sponsored by Rithm Capital Corp.
data.description: KBRA has assigned preliminary ratings to 10 classes of mortgage-backed notes from New Residential Mortgage Loan Trust 2026-NQM3, with a total transaction size of $475.8 million. This non-prime RMBS is sponsored by Rithm Capital Corp., previously known as New Residential Investment Corp., a publicly traded real estate investment trust listed on NYSE as RITM. The majority of the underlying mortgages (57.5%) in the pool were originated by NewRez LLC. It is important to note that all loans within this transaction will be serviced by New.

data.title: Coinbase stock rises despite mixed Q4 results; analysts lower price targets. JPMorgan cuts from $290 to $252, Rosenblatt from $325 to $240, Canaccord Genuity from $400 to $300, Benchmark from $421 to $267. Analysts optimistic about U.S. crypto legislation, see Coinbase as a key player in the crypto economy driven by product development and tokenization.
data.description: Coinbase Global (NASDAQ: COIN) stock rose as the company reported mixed Q4 results. Analysts lowered price targets in response, with JPMorgan lowering from $290 to $252, Rosenblatt from $325 to $240, Canaccord Genuity from $400 to $300, and Benchmark from $421 to $267. JPMorgan noted poor crypto environment affecting Q4 results, but remains optimistic about U.S. crypto legislation. Coinbase's focus on investment in crypto and stock buybacks shows conviction. Rosenblatt also acknowledges Q4 challenges but sees long-term success driven by product development and payments. The article provides more details on Benzinga.com.

data.title: SEGG Media acquires controlling interest in Veloce Media Group, unlocking $20M+ annual revenue. Closing on Feb 17.
data.description: The official date scheduled for the completion of the transaction is next Tuesday, February 17th.

data.title: Utility crews face compliance issues with fall protection standards due to Class 1 and 2 SRL distinctions.
data.description: A recent white paper released by FallTech delves into the impact of Class 1 and Class 2 SRL distinctions on work conditions within the electric utility sector. In Los Angeles, on Feb. 13, 2026, it was highlighted that numerous utility companies could potentially be non-compliant with existing fall protection regulations without their knowledge. This white paper sheds light on the issue and provides insights for organizations striving to align with safety standards, putting a focus on the importance of these distinctions in ensuring the safety of workers.

data.title: Black Diamond Capital Management acquires Palisades Center, to be operated by Spinoso Real Estate Group. (Characters: 112)
data.description: A shopping and entertainment center, Palisades Center, located in West Nyack, New York, has been acquired by Black Diamond Capital Management. The center will be operated by Spinoso Real Estate Group. The acquisition was made by investment funds managed by Black Diamond and was finalized on February 13, 2026. This marks a significant move in the industry, with the exact amount of the acquisition not disclosed at this time.

data.title: 15 global companies from 10 countries form Trusted Tech Alliance to serve customers worldwide, announced at Munich Security Conference.
data.description: 15 prominent multinational corporations representing 10 different nations have united to cater to a global customer base amidst a time of global division. The announcement was made today at the Munich Security Conference, with companies hailing from Africa, Asia, Europe, and North America collaborating to enhance their services worldwide.

data.title: CMS Energy declares dividend on 4.200% Cumulative Redeemable Perpetual Preferred Stock, with payment on Apr 15, 2026.
data.description: In Jackson, Michigan, on February 13, 2026, the Board of Directors of CMS Energy announced a dividend for the 4.200% Cumulative Redeemable Perpetual Preferred Stock, Series C. The dividend is scheduled to be paid on April 15, 2026, to shareholders who are recorded as such at the end of the specified period.

data.title: Deck Building Expert Scott LaDue from Victor, NY, warns of permit and setback pitfalls in backyard projects in a HelloNation article. #Regulations #Construction #VictorNY
data.description: In a recent article by HelloNation, Scott LaDue from Rochester Deck Builders in Victor, NY sheds light on the potential obstacles that could hinder a backyard construction project. Overlooking regulatory requirements is a common issue faced by homeowners, which may lead to delays or even cancellation of the project. LaDue emphasizes the importance of understanding setbacks and other essential regulations to ensure the construction process goes smoothly. By addressing these hidden challenges, homeowners can prevent disruptions and expedite their construction projects.

data.title: Beko CEO to step down after 11-year tenure; strategic continuity and sustainability to remain priorities.
data.description: Today, on February 13, 2026, Beko (Arçelik A.Ş.) revealed that Hakan Bulgurlu will be resigning as Chief Executive Officer after serving for 11 years. Bulgurlu's leadership saw the company navigate significant challenges and opportunities. This decision will be finalized at the next General Assembly meeting. Beko's stock is listed on Bloomberg under the symbol ARCLK TI.

data.title: PMG opens new offices in Mexico City & Toronto, plans to hire across various positions in both locations.
data.description: A global company, PMG, is set to expand its operations by opening two new offices in Mexico City, Mexico, and Toronto, Canada. The move will result in a significant number of job opportunities across various positions in both locations.

data.title: Walton Global introduces the American Builder Growth and Income Fund in Arizona, targeting both income and capital appreciation for builders.
data.description: Walton Global, a prominent company in real estate investment and land asset management, has introduced the American Builder Growth and Income Fund in Scottsdale, Arizona. This fund combines income-focused, secured land loans via Walton’s Builder Land Financing platform with a growth-centric land acquisition approach to capitalize on potential appreciation as developers purchase land. This strategy aims to address the fundamental undersupply in the U.S. housing market.

data.title: Retail enthusiasm for crypto is waning; Robinhood's earnings show 38% revenue drop in crypto transactions as crypto market fades from center stage. Bitcoin at $65,400 (46% below ATH) and Ethereum at $1,915 (60% below ATH). Retail trading in equities on the rise with individual traders making up 20% of total US stock trading volume. Institutional Bitcoin holdings increasing, retail interest in crypto declining. Overall crypto interest contracted by 28% in Q4.
data.description: Robinhood's earnings reflect a shift in retail trading behavior, with transaction-based revenue increasing by 15% year-over-year to $776 million, mainly driven by options and equities trading. However, cryptocurrency revenue decreased by 38% to $221 million. The shift away from crypto is evident as Bitcoin trades at around $65,400 (46% below its all-time high) and Ethereum at about $1,915 (60% below its peak). Retail participation remains strong in traditional markets, with individual traders accounting for 20% of total U.S. stock trading volume - close to the 2021 meme-stock surge. In contrast, interest in crypto is declining, with institutional Bitcoin holdings increasing while retail holdings decrease. Overall crypto-related interest contracted by 28% in Q4, indicating a cooling trend in wider participation. Retail investors now rival major institutional categories in equities trading, highlighting a significant shift in market participation dynamics.
